SYM Command@command.go:54 class
  SIG struct{...}
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Behavior is attached via ten nilable function-valued public fields on Command, not via methods."
    RATIONALE "Nilable function fields model optional hooks with zero boilerplate in the common case."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Cobra's core type must be a concrete struct with public fields, not an interface."
    RATIONALE "Struct-oriented shape enables declarative literal construction, which is the dominant idiom in Go and determines usability at scale."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Deprecated fields remain on the struct rather than being removed or renamed."
    RATIONALE "Renames are breaking changes to every user; deprecation proceeds by addition."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Dispatch must be a nil check on the field followed by a direct function call, not a method call on an interface value."
    RATIONALE "Direct function-field invocation avoids interface vtable overhead and matches the data model."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Evolution of Command must proceed by adding new fields, not changing or hiding existing ones."
    RATIONALE "Public fields are part of the semantic contract; renaming or hiding breaks every user."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Moving field access behind methods, introducing a Commander interface, or adding a registration contract is a breaking redesign, not a refactor."
    RATIONALE "Such changes would force every user of cobra to rewrite their command definitions."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "No Commander interface may be introduced for user command types."
    RATIONALE "Interface-based dispatch would force users into a second idiom and require optional-method detection via type assertions."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Registration is direct slice append/mutation via AddCommand/RemoveCommand; no registration hook, factory, or builder."
    RATIONALE "Direct struct mutation keeps registration simple and consistent with the data-oriented model."
  INTENT ADR-01 hard "Runnability is derived from data (Run != nil || RunE != nil), not declared through a type/interface."
    RATIONALE "Availability of behavior is computed from fields, preserving the data-oriented model."
  INTENT ADR-03 hard "By default (EnableTraverseRunHooks=false), only the nearest ancestor with a PersistentPreRun(E) hook fires; the chain walks child-to-root and breaks at the first defined hook."
    RATIONALE "Preserves backward compatibility with users who depended on override (not augment) semantics; running all ancestors would double-execute side effects."
  INTENT ADR-05 hard "The Command struct's method and field surface (e.g., .Commands, .LocalFlags, .HasAvailableSubCommands) is part of the public template API; renaming is a breaking change."
    RATIONALE "Templates reference these names directly; downstream custom templates would break on rename."
  INTENT ADR-06 hard "All flags in a group must be visible to a single Command; flag groups cannot cross-reference parent and child subcommands."
    RATIONALE "Group validation operates on a single Command's flag set after merging persistent flags."
  INTENT ADR-08 hard "Each Command carries three private IO fields (inReader, outWriter, errWriter) that are not directly accessible by users."
    RATIONALE "Private fields enforce the nil-means-inherit contract that the getter/walker logic depends on."
  INTENT docstring:args.go hard "ExactValidArgs is deprecated; use MatchAll(ExactArgs(n), OnlyValidArgs) instead"
    RATIONALE "Explicit godoc Deprecated: marker indicates the function should not be used in new code"
  INTENT ADR-01 soft "Derived state should be recomputed on access rather than cached, because there is no hook to invalidate caches when users mutate fields."
    RATIONALE "Users mutate public fields directly at any time, so cache invalidation has no hook to run on."
  INTENT ADR-08 soft "Tests should use a fresh rootCmd per test rather than reusing the same Command across subtests, to avoid stale writer state."
    RATIONALE "Reused commands retain prior writer configuration, risking test pollution."
  INTENT ADR-01 context "Cross-cutting middleware must be implemented via PersistentPreRun(E) or by attaching closures at construction; no middleware-wrapping hook exists."
    RATIONALE "A consequence of the struct model: no shared interface to hook against."
  INTENT ADR-01 context "Doc generation and completion read struct fields directly rather than going through accessor methods."
    RATIONALE "Keeping commands as data makes metadata accessible without bloating an interface contract."
  INTENT ADR-01 context "Internal methods on *Command are permitted; the rule governs only the user-facing command definition shape."
    RATIONALE "Clarifies the scope of the constraint — structs with fields constructed literally is what must be preserved."
  INTENT ADR-01 context "Misconfiguration is detected at runtime, not compile time; users needing stronger guarantees must add their own validation."
